verses.latin_vulgate: Omiserunt autem tres viri isti respondere Job, eo quod justus sibi videretur.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַֽיִּשְׁבְּת֡וּ שְׁלֹ֤שֶׁת הָאֲנָשִׁ֣ים הָ֭אֵלֶּה מֵעֲנ֣וֹת אֶת־אִיּ֑וֹב כִּ֤י ה֖וּא צַדִּ֣יק בְּעֵינָֽיו׃ {פ}
verses.king_james: So these three men ceased to answer Job, because he was righteous in his own eyes.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et iratus indignatusque est Eliu filius Barachel Buzites, de cognatione Ram : iratus est autem adversum Job, eo quod justum se esse diceret coram Deo.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּ֤חַר אַ֨ף ׀ אֱלִיה֣וּא בֶן־בַּרַכְאֵ֣ל הַבּוּזִי֮ מִמִּשְׁפַּ֢חַ֫ת רָ֥ם בְּ֭אִיּוֹב חָרָ֣ה אַפּ֑וֹ עַֽל־צַדְּק֥וֹ נַ֝פְשׁ֗וֹ מֵאֱלֹהִֽים׃
verses.king_james: Then was kindled the wrath of Elihu the son of Barachel the Buzite, of the kindred of Ram: against Job was his wrath kindled, because he justified himself rather than God.

verses.latin_vulgate: Porro adversum amicos ejus indignatus est, eo quod non invenissent responsionem rationabilem, sed tantummodo condemnassent Job.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וּבִשְׁלֹ֣שֶׁת רֵעָיו֮ חָרָ֢ה אַ֫פּ֥וֹ עַ֤ל אֲשֶׁ֣ר לֹא־מָצְא֣וּ מַעֲנֶ֑ה וַ֝יַּרְשִׁ֗יעוּ אֶת־אִיּֽוֹב׃
verses.king_james: Also against his three friends was his wrath kindled, because they had found no answer, and yet had condemned Job.

verses.latin_vulgate: Igitur Eliu expectavit Job loquentem, eo quod seniores essent qui loquebantur.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וֶאֱלִיה֗וּ חִכָּ֣ה אֶת־אִ֭יּוֹב בִּדְבָרִ֑ים כִּ֤י זְֽקֵנִים־הֵ֖מָּה מִמֶּ֣נּוּ לְיָמִֽים׃
verses.king_james: Now Elihu had waited till Job had spoken, because they were elder than he.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cum autem vidisset quod tres respondere non potuissent, iratus est vehementer.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּ֤רְא אֱלִיה֗וּא כִּ֘י־אֵ֤ין מַעֲנֶ֗ה בְּ֭פִי שְׁלֹ֥שֶׁת הָאֲנָשִׁ֗ים וַיִּ֥חַר אַפּֽוֹ׃ {פ}
verses.king_james: When Elihu saw that there was no answer in the mouth of these three men, then his wrath was kindled.

verses.latin_vulgate: Respondensque Eliu filius Barachel Buzites, dixit :   Junior sum tempore, vos autem antiquiores :  idcirco, demisso capite,  veritus sum vobis indicare meam sententiam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּ֤עַן ׀ אֱלִיה֖וּא בֶן־בַּרַכְאֵ֥ל הַבּוּזִ֗י וַיֹּ֫אמַ֥ר צָ֘עִ֤יר אֲנִ֣י לְ֭יָמִים וְאַתֶּ֣ם יְשִׁישִׁ֑ים עַל־כֵּ֖ן זָחַ֥לְתִּי וָאִירָ֓א ׀ מֵחַוֺּ֖ת דֵּעִ֣י אֶתְכֶֽם׃
verses.king_james: And Elihu the son of Barachel the Buzite answered and said, I am young, and ye are very old; wherefore I was afraid, and durst not shew you mine opinion.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sperabam enim quod ætas prolixior loqueretur,  et annorum multitudo doceret sapientiam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אָ֭מַרְתִּי יָמִ֣ים יְדַבֵּ֑רוּ וְרֹ֥ב שָׁ֝נִ֗ים יֹדִ֥יעוּ חׇכְמָֽה׃
verses.king_james: I said, Days should speak, and multitude of years should teach wisdom.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sed, ut video, spiritus est in hominibus,  et inspiratio Omnipotentis dat intelligentiam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אָ֭כֵן רוּחַ־הִ֣יא בֶאֱנ֑וֹשׁ וְנִשְׁמַ֖ת שַׁדַּ֣י תְּבִינֵֽם׃
verses.king_james: But there is a spirit in man: and the inspiration of the Almighty giveth them understanding.

verses.latin_vulgate: Non sunt longævi sapientes,  nec senes intelligunt judicium.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לֹא־רַבִּ֥ים יֶחְכָּ֑מוּ וּ֝זְקֵנִ֗ים יָבִ֥ינוּ מִשְׁפָּֽט׃
verses.king_james: Great men are not always wise: neither do the aged understand judgment.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ideo dicam : Audite me :  ostendam vobis etiam ego meam sapientiam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לָכֵ֣ן אָ֭מַרְתִּי שִׁמְעָה־לִּ֑י אֲחַוֶּ֖ה דֵעִ֣י אַף־אָֽנִי׃
verses.king_james: Therefore I said, Hearken to me; I also will shew mine opinion.

verses.latin_vulgate: Expectavi enim sermones vestros ;  audivi prudentiam vestram,  donec disceptaremini sermonibus ;
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הֵ֤ן הוֹחַ֨לְתִּי ׀ לְֽדִבְרֵיכֶ֗ם אָ֭זִין עַד־תְּב֥וּנֹֽתֵיכֶ֑ם עַֽד־תַּחְקְר֥וּן מִלִּֽין׃
verses.king_james: Behold, I waited for your words; I gave ear to your reasons, whilst ye searched out what to say.

verses.latin_vulgate: et donec putabam vos aliquid dicere, considerabam :  sed, ut video, non est qui possit arguere Job,  et respondere ex vobis sermonibus ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְעָ֥דֵיכֶ֗ם אֶתְבּ֫וֹנָ֥ן וְהִנֵּ֤ה אֵ֣ין לְאִיּ֣וֹב מוֹכִ֑יחַ עוֹנֶ֖ה אֲמָרָ֣יו מִכֶּֽם׃
verses.king_james: Yea, I attended unto you, and, behold, there was none of you that convinced Job, or that answered his words:

verses.latin_vulgate: Ne forte dicatis : Invenimus sapientiam :  Deus projecit eum, non homo.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: פֶּן־תֹּ֣֭אמְרוּ מָצָ֣אנוּ חׇכְמָ֑ה אֵ֖ל יִדְּפֶ֣נּוּ לֹא־אִֽישׁ׃
verses.king_james: Lest ye should say, We have found out wisdom: God thrusteth him down, not man.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nihil locutus est mihi :  et ego non secundum sermones vestros respondebo illi.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְלֹא־עָרַ֣ךְ אֵלַ֣י מִלִּ֑ין וּ֝בְאִמְרֵיכֶ֗ם לֹ֣א אֲשִׁיבֶֽנּוּ׃
verses.king_james: Now he hath not directed his words against me: neither will I answer him with your speeches.

verses.latin_vulgate: Extimuerunt, nec responderunt ultra,  abstuleruntque a se eloquia.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: חַ֭תּוּ לֹא־עָ֣נוּ ע֑וֹד הֶעְתִּ֖יקוּ מֵהֶ֣ם מִלִּֽים׃
verses.king_james: They were amazed, they answered no more: they left off speaking.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quoniam igitur expectavi, et non sunt locuti :  steterunt, nec ultra responderunt :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְ֭הוֹחַלְתִּי כִּי־לֹ֣א יְדַבֵּ֑רוּ כִּ֥י עָ֝מְד֗וּ לֹא־עָ֥נוּ עֽוֹד׃
verses.king_james: When I had waited, (for they spake not, but stood still, and answered no more;)

verses.latin_vulgate: respondebo et ego partem meam,  et ostendam scientiam meam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אַעֲנֶ֣ה אַף־אֲנִ֣י חֶלְקִ֑י אֲחַוֶּ֖ה דֵעִ֣י אַף־אָֽנִי׃
verses.king_james: I said, I will answer also my part, I also will shew mine opinion.

verses.latin_vulgate: Plenus sum enim sermonibus,  et coarctat me spiritus uteri mei.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֭י מָלֵ֣תִי מִלִּ֑ים הֱ֝צִיקַ֗תְנִי ר֣וּחַ בִּטְנִֽי׃
verses.king_james: For I am full of matter, the spirit within me constraineth me.

verses.latin_vulgate: En venter meus quasi mustum absque spiraculo,  quod lagunculas novas disrumpit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הִנֵּֽה־בִטְנִ֗י כְּיַ֥יִן לֹֽא־יִפָּתֵ֑חַ כְּאֹב֥וֹת חֲ֝דָשִׁ֗ים יִבָּקֵֽעַ׃
verses.king_james: Behold, my belly is as wine which hath no vent; it is ready to burst like new bottles.

verses.latin_vulgate: Loquar, et respirabo paululum :  aperiam labia mea, et respondebo.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֲדַבְּרָ֥ה וְיִֽרְוַֽח־לִ֑י אֶפְתַּ֖ח שְׂפָתַ֣י וְאֶעֱנֶֽה׃
verses.king_james: I will speak, that I may be refreshed: I will open my lips and answer.

verses.latin_vulgate: Non accipiam personam viri,  et Deum homini non æquabo.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אַל־נָ֭א אֶשָּׂ֣א פְנֵי־אִ֑ישׁ וְאֶל־אָ֝דָ֗ם לֹ֣א אֲכַנֶּֽה׃
verses.king_james: Let me not, I pray you, accept any man’s person, neither let me give flattering titles unto man.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nescio enim quamdiu subsistam,  et si post modicum tollat me factor meus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֤י לֹ֣א יָדַ֣עְתִּי אֲכַנֶּ֑ה כִּ֝מְעַ֗ט יִשָּׂאֵ֥נִי עֹשֵֽׂנִי׃
verses.king_james: For I know not to give flattering titles; in so doing my maker would soon take me away.
